name: Shannon MacLeod, Gillibrand 2020
subject: Update on our plan to win the summer: New Hampshire edition!
body:

| |
---
Peter, really quick! You heard from Lara this morning about Kirsten barnstorming Iowa, and from Jacob yesterday about our plan to win the summer.
In a minute, I'm going to tell you about the other crucial piece of this--New Hampshire--but first, I just heard we're falling a bit behind where we need to be in order to execute all of our summer plans. **[If we bring in $10,000 by midnight tonight, I think we can make up the difference. Can you make a donation right now to help strengthen our early states organizing operation?](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f977442/2ae112ef/698b993e/357c694b/3872416530/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMxMzUtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3135-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
I wanted to send you a quick note from New Hampshire today because having a strong performance here in the Granite State--the first primary in the nation--will say so much about the strength of our campaign.
That's why Kirsten is going to spend a ton of time here in July, crisscrossing the state!
And we have plans to open a second field office here soon and staff up big time to make sure our organizers can be in a million places at once, growing support for our campaign.
But we can only do all this with your help.
**Can you donate to help hit our $10,000 goal before midnight tonight so we can get Kirsten to New Hampshire and build a strong on-the-ground organizing program? Here 's the best link:**
**[https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f977442/2ae112ef/698b993e/357c694b/3872416530/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMxMzUtZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3135-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
Thanks so much--we're going to win the summer with your help!

Shannon MacLeod
New Hampshire State Director

name: Governor Bill Weld | Weld 2020
subject: Crisis of Trust
body:
| [ ](http://www.weld2020.org/?e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=kennedy6&n=1)
---
Dear Friend --

It is no wonder that Americans are experiencing a CRISIS OF TRUST in the presidency. We have a President who lies to the people, obstructs justice and cares more about how to personally profit from the Executive Office than he does about the American people. _[I’m running for President because I know that if we stand together, we can build a Better America, the kind of America our children deserve, but I NEED YOUR HELP. Can you give $5, $10 or $20 TODAY to help defeat this President?](https://www.weld2020.org/donate?e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=kennedy6&n=2)_ The other night, in a speech at the Kennedy Institute in Boston, I called for a number of reforms that would help restore our faith in the foundational institutions of our democracy _. Reforms that would hold our elected representatives accountable to YOU – the people.
_
The most important reform we must address immediately is to pass a law that would require presidential candidates to share at least five years of their tax returns when running for office and then every year while serving.

**Trust is built on transparency** and the American people deserve to know whether or not their President is dealing with foreign adversaries or profiting off of the Oval Office. The President of the United States should not be allowed to hide his dirty dealings from the people.

**[I need you to stand with me. I need your help. Together, we can make sure that this President does not get the chance to lie, cheat and obstruct his way to a second term. Please give $10, $25 or $40 today.](https://www.weld2020.org/donate?e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=kennedy6&n=3)

** Please give today. YOU can make a difference! Together, WE can make a Better America!

Thank you,
Bill

This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://www.weld2020.org/unsubscribe?e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=kennedy6&n=4).
You can also keep up with Weld 2020 on [Twitter](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=7Sn_jlQ3J2wHxMyMtp8UNEee0J3CzZBj8v0TKy2tYMM&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=kennedy6&n=5) or [Facebook](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6sD5mbawGI5P2loKSMnHaUPJQdagn1SdGMQUy6UvfZqO&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=kennedy6&n=6).
Created with [NationBuilder](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=SWqLExi-4iBlEOa5qgO58jA2d62deOsOLwOgchKDecY&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=kennedy6&n=7), software for leaders.

name: Cory Booker
subject: Listen to the people
body:
| | |
---
| Friend, In a moment, I'm going to [**_ask you for a donation to my campaign for president, but first I want to tell you a story from the campaign trail._**](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190524-em?refcode=em_fr_20190525_CB_20190525-fr-CB-listening-actives_actives_l1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Last week in Littleton, New Hampshire, a high school sophomore pulled me aside to talk about solutions to the opioid crisis in her hometown. I am so honored I was able to hear her story and have the opportunity to take the time to listen to her ideas about how we solve this epidemic. **It is striking that we 're living in a time where a candidate actually _listening_ to the people made news. (Seriously. It made headlines -- [**_check it out_**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0945/5fca0d19/e4316e/291b6265/3993321099/VEsE/ "https://twitter.com/CoryBooker/status/1128677500677128193").) At the end of the day, we don't live in an autocracy. The presidency belongs to the people.** Listening to what people think shouldn't be an anomaly -- even if they're not old enough to vote yet. If we truly want to solve the big issues our country faces, whether it's the opioid crisis or making sure all communities have clean water, we've got to listen to the people who are living through these experiences. We need to talk to each other and work together -- and that's what this campaign has always been about. **Now, we 're coming up on a pivotal moment in this race. The debates start _next month_ and because ours is an underdog campaign, we need to go into June with as much momentum as possible.** [**_Will you make a donation to my campaign before the end of May deadline to help us start strong in this next big phase?_**](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190524-em?refcode=em_fr_20190525_CB_20190525-fr-CB-listening-actives_actives_l2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=") Hope to chat with you out on the trail sometime soon, Cory [](https://act.corybooker. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cory2020-20190524-em?refcode=em_fr_20190525_CB_20190525-fr-CB-listening-actives_actives_contribbutton&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

---
| | Paid for by Cory 2020
---

This campaign is moving quickly. If you want the most up-to-date information, follow Cory on [Instagram](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0945/5fca0d19/e4316e/291b6261/3993321099/VEsA/ "https://www.instagram.com/corybooker/"), [Twitter](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0945/5fca0d19/e4316e/291b6260/3993321099/VEsB/ "https://twitter.com/corybooker"), and [Facebook](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0945/5fca0d19/e4316e/291b626f/3993321099/VEsO/ "https://www.facebook.com/corybooker").
It's time for leadership that can reignite our spirit of common purpose. That takes all of us. We believe these emails are an important way to stay in touch, but if at any point you'd like to stop getting these updates, you can unsubscribe [here](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0945/5fca0d19/e4316e/291b626e/3993321099/VEsP/ "https://act.corybooker.com/unsubscribe").

name: Kamala Harris
subject: I am really enjoying running for president
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
I don’t know what it says about my personality, but I am really enjoying running for president! I'm not getting enough sleep, and I sometimes don't know when we’ll have time to stop and grab a bite to eat. **But there is nothing more powerful than being on the road across the country, listening to the people who are sharing their story with me and with our country.** These are people who are taking time out of their day -- often on a weekend -- because they love our country, and they’re prepared to fight for it. There's so much worth fighting for -- and this is a fight that is born out of optimism. **That’s the part of the job that I love. The part I _don’t_ love so much is asking for donations. But it needs to be done. Here’s why:** Our campaign is not accepting a dime from corporate PACs or federal lobbyists. That means we are leaving money on the table -- but it was the right choice to make. We’ve had enough of our democracy being corrupted by the highest bidder. Together, we are putting the power back in the hands of the people. But that means we really, truly do count on people like you pitching in to emails just like this one to power our movement. And that’s why I’m asking again today: **Will you help me out with the part of the job I don’t love the most -- fundraising -- so I can do the part I love most: staying out on the campaign trail, meeting with supporters, and hearing their stories? If you can afford to, please add a donation today to help us reach our end-of-month fundraising goal:** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $5 now](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190525-eom?refcode=ema190525-eom8-a&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=FL&zip=34223&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&express_lane=true&t=1&refcode2=5172_5755789_biA239&akid=5172%2E5755789%2EbiA239)

subject: What Elizabeth is up to this weekend...

body:
Here's the catch: Campaigns are expensive. By swearing off private fundraisers with wealthy donors, we're leaving money on the table. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fb79/45c30650/9d321c42/6699f543/2427648641/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
Here's a glimpse behind the curtain that a lot of other campaigns don't want you to see:

On a long weekend like this one, candidates scoop money from mega-wealthy donors. They shuttle around to barbecues in the Hamptons and swanky dinners in Los Angeles -- all behind closed doors.

**And what do you need to do to get inside and have special access to a presidential candidate? Write a big, big, check. (Tip: the more zeroes, the better.)

[You won't see that on this campaign. Elizabeth doesn't devote any time in her schedule just for big donors. We all own an equal piece of this campaign no matter the amount we can contribute. Can you chip in to our campaign and help grow our movement to get big money out of politics?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fb79/45c30650/9d321c42/6699f542/2427648641/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW1heTE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNTI1ZW0xLXYxczIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-may19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190525em1-v1s2")**

This weekend, Elizabeth is heading back to Iowa -- not to court rich donors and pick up big checks, but to attend house parties and host organizing meetings that are open to the public.

That's because if we want to make big, structural change in this country, we need to spend time talking face to face with people all across the country to grow a movement big enough to win.

Here's the catch: Campaigns are expensive. By swearing off private fundraisers with wealthy donors, we're leaving money on the table.

But this is the campaign we want to run. Are you in?

**[Chip in to help build a grassroots campaign that will prove we can get big money out of politics and still win.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852fb79/45c30650/9d321c42/6699f542/2427648641/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW1heTE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNTI1ZW0xLXYxczIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-may19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190525em1-v1s2")**

clean_body: Here's the catch: Campaigns are expensive. By swearing off private fundraisers with wealthy donors, we're leaving money on the table. | | | "" Here's a glimpse behind the curtain that a lot of other campaigns don't want you to see: On a long weekend like this one, candidates scoop money from mega-wealthy donors. They shuttle around to barbecues in the Hamptons and swanky dinners in Los Angeles -- all behind closed doors. And what do you need to do to get inside and have special access to a presidential candidate? Write a big, big, check. (Tip: the more zeroes, the better.) "" This weekend, Elizabeth is heading back to Iowa -- not to court rich donors and pick up big checks, but to attend house parties and host organizing meetings that are open to the public. That's because if we want to make big, structural change in this country, we need to spend time talking face to face with people all across the country to grow a movement big enough to win. Here's the catch: Campaigns are expensive. By swearing off private fundraisers with wealthy donors, we're leaving money on the table. But this is the campaign we want to run. Are you in? "" Thanks, Team Warren | "" | | | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" | "" ---|---|---|---|---|--- | Paid for by Warren for President All content (C) 2019 Warren for President, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02116

In the several months since officially kicking off my campaign, I've launched bold policy proposals and spoken out strongly on the issues that matter--and I want to make sure I can keep doing that from the debate stage.
I detailed my Clean Elections Plan to get dark money out of politics and return political power where it belongs: in the hands of the people.
Along with millions of Americans, I spoke out against Republican attacks on reproductive rights, and I traveled to Georgia to lift up the voices of the women who will be hit the hardest by that state's draconian, unconstitutional abortion ban.
And just this past week, I released my Family Bill of Rights. It's a major economic policy proposal I'll get done in my first 100 days as president to ease the financial burden on families and level the playing field for America's kids.
**I believe I deserve the chance to stand on that Democratic debate stage and say my piece about these policies and so much more.**
But the DNC's new rules have put my chance at guaranteeing my spot in jeopardy, and with just a month to go until the first debate, I need your help.
**Become one of the 65,000 donors I need to help guarantee my spot on the debate stage, where I promise I 'll be the strongest voice for women, families, and transparency and accountability:**

Thanks for your support, today and always.

Kirsten

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
"The American people are sending a pretty clear message to Washington: They are sick and tired of the investigations into President Trump." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7837340e92&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [Americans Don't Want Impeachment, but Democrats Aren't Listening](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=48f6618eb2&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“The American people are sending a pretty clear message to Washington: They are sick and tired of the investigations into President Trump and don't want Democrats in Congress to impeach him,” Marc Thiessen of the American Enterprise Institute writes. “A Harvard-Harris poll finds that 65 percent of Americans say Congress should not begin impeachment proceedings against Trump . . . And 58 percent believe that ‘Given the Mueller report ... we should turn the page on investigations of President Trump.’”

**🎬****Press Secretary Sarah Sanders:** [“At some point . . . it’s time to move on.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=897f714982&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [Pelosi, Dems Just Don't Care About Our National Emergency](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e6e49e59d7&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“Now in the majority with control over the House floor, Speaker Nancy Pelosi and her conference are poised to let funding for the Unaccompanied Alien Children (UAC) program run out . . . First, Pelosi and Democrats denied the existence of the national emergency. Now, they are willing to let federal agencies run out of money to care for migrant children,” Reps. Chip Roy (R-TX), Andy Biggs (R-AZ), Jim Jordan (R-OH) write.

** Something to share:** [Our brave Border Patrol officers continue to save lives](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bde95bd8ab&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**Americans Feel Good About Economy and Give Trump Credit – CBS News Poll**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ffb86105a2&e=3ee4985eea)
-CBS News
“Most Americans remain confident about the U.S. economy and in their own financial situation,” CBS News reports. “Seven in 10 Americans say the economy is in good shape, including a quarter who say it is very good. Nearly all Republicans and most Democrats and independents share this view.” Additionally, 85 percent of Americans who rate the economy as good said the President’s policies are mostly or partially responsible.
---
|
---
| [Melania Makes Special Trip to Walter Reed to Present Purple Heart](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cb3209501c&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Daily Caller_
“First lady Melania Trump made a special trip Wednesday to Walter Reed National Military Medical Center in Bethesda, Maryland, to meet with ‘wounded warriors’ and present a Purple Heart,” Katie Jerkovich reports. “As each of them recovers, we are reminded of the cost of our country’s security. I am grateful,” the First Lady said.

**In photos:** [First Lady Melania Trump visits Walter Reed Medical Center](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=893d01d732&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [Delaying Approval of New Trade Deal Hurts American Workers, Families and the Economy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8e777029be&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Hill_
“The U.S. will be losing out on more jobs, more customers for Made-in-America goods and a stronger economy unless the U.S. House of Representatives takes up the updated trade agreement with Mexico and Canada,” Reps. Kevin Brady (R-TX) and Steve Scalise (R-LA) write. “Paychecks are rising, especially among blue-collar and low-income workers. America’s manufacturing is back . . . Delaying USMCA means fewer economic benefits to hard working Americans.”
---
|
---
| [Dems Tied to Obama’s Pro-Iran ‘Echo Chamber’ Spread Terror Regime’s Anti-Trump Talking Points](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=146af00700&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Washington Free Beacon_
“A cadre of former top Obama administration figures and their allies in Congress have been working to amplify anti-Trump talking points issued by a senior Iranian regime official in what sources have described as an effort by Obama's pro-Iran echo chamber to drive a wedge between President Donald Trump and his national security team,” Adam Kredo writes. Democrats are “doing all they can to subvert Trump, even if it means siding with the Iranian regime in the standoff.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5962dcd7eb&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b1dfae8ed1&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=9dee3dc1ba)
---

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Walmart
body:
The richest family in America is not going to like this.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-)

**Walmart workers who are sick and tired of being paid poverty wages invited me to attend their company’s shareholders meeting.

I will tell the Walton family — who are worth more than $170 billion — to end their greed, pay their workers a living wage, and put hourly Walmart workers on the company’s board. And I need to have your support before I go.

Add your name to say you stand with Walmart workers before my address in Arkansas.
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em_walmart_wage/?source=em190525-1-45&refcode2=1463.150750.NVoZD-&t=2&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-)
**

* * *
Friends -
In a couple weeks, I will attend Walmart’s annual shareholder meeting in Arkansas.
At this meeting, I will directly address the Walton family — the richest family in America — and other Walmart shareholders about their wealth, their greed, their mistreatment of workers, and their exploitation of American taxpayers.
The Walton family, which owns Walmart, is worth more than $170 billion. Yet many Walmart workers are making wages so low that many of them are forced to rely on food stamps, Medicaid and public housing to survive — paid for by U.S. taxpayers. That should not be happening at a company owned by the wealthiest family in America.
Walmart workers are sick and tired of this. So a group of them invited me to speak on their behalf at the Walmart shareholders meeting, where I will present the workers’ common-sense proposal to give hourly employees of Walmart a seat on the company’s board of directors. The people who make the company successful deserve to have a seat at the table.
I am honored to be asked to speak for these workers. But this a moment that is about much more than Bernie Sanders.
**When I tell the richest family in America about a solution to their endless greed, I want to have as many supporters as possible giving their voice in solidarity with Walmart workers.** That is why I am asking you today:
**[Add your name if you agree: the Walton family and Walmart need to give Walmart workers a living wage of at least $15 an hour and include hourly employees on the company’s board of directors. It is important that I have your support before I travel to Arkansas. Will you add your name?**](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em_walmart_wage/?source=em190525-1-45&refcode2=1463.150750.NVoZD-&t=3&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-)
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em_walmart_wage/?source=em190525-1-45&refcode2=1463.150750.NVoZD-&t=4&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-)
These workers are not asking for much. All they want is a living wage so that they can pay the bills, put food on the table, send their kids to college and save for retirement. They want a union. They want to have a seat on corporate boards. And they want to end corporate greed that is destroying the social fabric of America.
If hourly workers at Walmart were well represented on its board, I doubt you would see the CEO of Walmart making over a thousand times more than the average worker. I also doubt you would see Walmart spending $20 billion on stock buybacks when it could be using that money to pay its workers a middle class wage with good benefits.
Walmart is not a poor company. It is not going broke. Last year, it made nearly $10 billion in profits.
Rob Walton, the eldest son of Walmart’s founder, spent an estimated $226 million on an antique car collection that includes 12 Ferraris, six Porsches, two Maseratis, and a 1963 Corvette Grand Sport Roadster.
His sister Alice, another heir to the Walmart fortune, had no problem amassing a private art collection worth an estimated $500 million, buying a $44 million painting, purchasing a $25 million two-floor condo on New York’s Park Avenue with 52 windows overlooking Central Park, or acquiring a $22 million 4,400-acre ranch in Texas.
When I am in Arkansas speaking on behalf of Walmart workers, I will be in the room, speaking directly to Waltons like Rob and Alice and the rest of the richest family in America.
And I will be telling them: Get off of corporate welfare, pay your workers a living wage of at least $15 an hour and include hourly employees on Walmart’s board of directors.
I don’t want the Waltons to just think they’re hearing from Bernie Sanders. When I speak to the Waltons, I want them to hear the voices of Walmart workers — and I want them to hear from thousands of people like you who agree that it is time to end the reckless greed of their family, their corporation, and that of other greedy corporations in the country.
So please.
**[If you agree with me that Walmart needs to respect its workers, it’s important that you add your name before I go to Arkansas and deliver my message to the Waltons. Please add your name here.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em_walmart_wage/?source=em190525-1-45&refcode2=1463.150750.NVoZD-&t=5&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-)
It’s not a radical idea to say that if you’re a profitable corporation like Walmart, you must pay all of your workers a living wage with good benefits before you go out and reward wealthy executives.
The fight of these Walmart employees is the fight of all of us. Thank you for standing with us.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/em_walmart_wage/?source=em190525-1-45&refcode2=1463.150750.NVoZD-&t=6&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=7&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=8&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=9&refcode2=1463_150750_NVoZD-&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=10&akid=1463%2E150750%2ENVoZD-).

body:
[1]Beto for America

Today marks two months since I joined Beto’s team! We’ve already made
major progress on hiring, rolling out our first big policy announcements,
and building infrastructure for the months to come.

But this campaign won’t be won or lost by the staff we hire, the websites
we build, or the offices we open. We’ll win by building the largest
grassroots effort in the country, harnessing the ingenuity, power, and
excitement of millions of Americans fighting for a better future.

That’s why we set a big goal this week to hit 25,000 more donations by the
end of the month. Our fundraising has to come from the people. But
realistically we’re going to need to increase our pace to make it to our
goal by May 31.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17598?refcode=ema20190525-32686&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1019&akid=32686%2E444211%2EUTA_dl ]Can you make a donation of $3 right now to help us
reach our goal? Your donations ensure we can run a strong grassroots
campaign with donations from ALL people. No PACs, corporations, or special
interests.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17598?refcode=ema20190525-32686&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=32686%2E444211%2EUTA_dl ]Contribute $3 »

This week I had my first chance to see Beto in Iowa. The crowds at each
event, the thoughtful questions from Iowans, and the organizers working
hard on the ground left me feeling more hopeful than ever. We will win if
we build this campaign together.

It’s an honor to be in this with you.

Jen O’Malley Dillon
Campaign Manager
Beto for America

body:
Yesterday you heard from my team about my upcoming re-election campaign launch.

**I am still so unbelievably grateful for the outpouring of support I received from grassroots supporters like you during my last campaign. **

But I am going to need your help again for my re-election campaign because Special Interests are already targeting Arizona as the _most important state_ in the 2020 election, and they are lining up to attack me.
